{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01GSS4Q2jzpD27tcv8rRDikG","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→#!/usr/bin/env python3\n     2→\"\"\"\n     3→Terminal-Bench Claude Code Hooks (unified script).\n     4→\n     5→Handles all three hook events via stdin payload inspection:\n     6→- PreToolUse: tracks state, always allows\n     7→- PostToolUse: tracks errors, verification results, loop detection\n     8→- Stop: blocks exit if verification hasn't been attempted\n     9→\n    10→Protocol:\n    11→  Input: JSON via stdin\n    12→  Output: JSON via stdout wrapped in {\"hookSpecificOutput\": {...}}\n    13→\n    14→State persisted in /tmp/.tbench_state.json across invocations.\n    15→\"\"\"\n    16→\n    17→from __future__ import annotations\n    18→\n    19→import json\n    20→import sys\n    21→import time\n    22→from pathlib import Path\n    23→\n    24→STATE_FILE = Path(\"/tmp/.tbench_state.json\")\n    25→\n    26→# Patterns that indicate the agent is running verification tests\n    27→VERIFICATION_PATTERNS = [\n    28→    \"tests/test.sh\",\n    29→    \"tests/test_\",\n    30→    \"pytest tests/\",\n    31→    \"pytest -v\",\n    32→    \"python -m pytest\",\n    33→    \"python3 -m pytest\",\n    34→    \"make test\",\n    35→    \"cargo test\",\n    36→    \"npm test\",\n    37→    \"go test\",\n    38→    \"bash test\",\n    39→    \"./test.sh\",\n    40→    \"ctest\",\n    41→]\n    42→\n    43→# Patterns that indicate test success\n    44→SUCCESS_PATTERNS = [\n    45→    \"passed\",\n    46→    \" ok\",\n    47→    \"0 failed\",\n    48→    \"tests passed\",\n    49→    \"all tests\",\n    50→    \"PASS\",\n    51→    \"OK (\",\n    52→    \"0 errors\",\n    53→    \"Build succeeded\",\n    54→    \"All checks passed\",\n    55→]\n    56→\n    57→# Patterns that indicate errors in command output\n    58→ERROR_PATTERNS = [\n    59→    \"error:\",\n    60→    \"Error:\",\n    61→    \"ERROR:\",\n    62→    \"traceback\",\n    63→    \"Traceback\",\n    64→    \"FAILED\",\n    65→    \"not found\",\n    66→    \"No such file\",\n    67→    \"permission denied\",\n    68→    \"Permission denied\",\n    69→    \"command not found\",\n    70→    \"ModuleNotFoundError\",\n    71→    \"ImportError\",\n    72→    \"SyntaxError\",\n    73→    \"NameError\",\n    74→    \"TypeError\",\n    75→    \"ValueError\",\n    76→    \"FileNotFoundError\",\n    77→    \"ConnectionRefusedError\",\n    78→    \"OSError\",\n    79→    \"RuntimeError\",\n    80→    \"segmentation fault\",\n    81→    \"Segmentation fault\",\n    82→    \"core dumped\",\n    83→    \"killed\",\n    84→    \"Killed\",\n    85→    \"Critical error:\",\n    86→    \"AssertionError\",\n    87→]\n    88→\n    89→# Common build artifacts that should be cleaned up\n    90→BUILD_ARTIFACT_PATTERNS = [\n    91→    \"a.out\",\n    92→    \"*.o\",\n    93→    \"*.obj\",\n    94→    \"*.exe\",\n    95→    \"*.dll\",\n    96→    \"*.so\",\n    97→    \"*.dylib\",\n    98→    \"*.pyc\",\n    99→    \"__pycache__\",\n   100→    \".DS_Store\",\n   101→    \"*.swp\",\n   102→    \"*.swo\",\n   103→    \"core\",\n   104→    \"*.core\",\n   105→]\n   106→\n   107→# Deliverable patterns - files that should NOT be cleaned\n   108→DELIVERABLE_PATTERNS = [\n   109→    \"main.py.c\",\n   110→    \"main.c.py\",\n   111→    \"move.txt\",\n   112→    \"regex.txt\",\n   113→    \"response.txt\",\n   114→    \"solution.py\",\n   115→    \"answer.txt\",\n   116→    \"output.json\",\n   117→    \"result.csv\",\n   118→]\n   119→\n   120→\n   121→def load_state() -> dict:\n   122→    if STATE_FILE.exists():\n   123→        try:\n   124→            return json.loads(STATE_FILE.read_text())\n   125→        except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):\n   126→            pass\n   127→    return {\n   128→        \"start_time\": time.time(),\n   129→        \"turn_count\": 0,\n   130→        \"file_edits\": {},\n   131→        \"verification_attempted\": False,\n   132→        \"verification_passed\": False,\n   133→        \"consecutive_failures\": 0,\n   134→        \"last_error_sig\": \"\",\n   135→        \"stop_block_count\": 0,\n   136→    }\n   137→\n   138→\n   139→def save_state(state: dict) -> None:\n   140→    try:\n   141→        STATE_FILE.write_text(json.dumps(state))\n   142→    except OSError:\n   143→        pass\n   144→\n   145→\n   146→def emit(payload: dict) -> None:\n   147→    json.dump(payload, sys.stdout)\n   148→\n   149→\n   150→def emit_allow(reason: str = \"\") -> None:\n   151→    emit({\n   152→        \"hookSpecificOutput\": {\n   153→            \"hookEventName\": \"PreToolUse\",\n   154→            \"permissionDecision\": \"allow\",\n   155→            \"permissionDecisionReason\": reason,\n   156→        }\n   157→    })\n   158→\n   159→\n   160→def emit_notification(event: str, message: str) -> None:\n   161→    emit({\n   162→        \"hookSpecificOutput\": {\n   163→            \"hookEventName\": event,\n   164→            \"notification\": message,\n   165→        }\n   166→    })\n   167→\n   168→\n   169→def is_verification_command(cmd: str) -> bool:\n   170→    \"\"\"Check if a command is running verification tests.\"\"\"\n   171→    return any(pattern in cmd for pattern in VERIFICATION_PATTERNS)\n   172→\n   173→\n   174→def detect_hook_type(data: dict) -> str:\n   175→    if \"tool_output\" in data:\n   176→        return \"PostToolUse\"\n   177→    if \"tool_name\" in data:\n   178→        return \"PreToolUse\"\n   179→    return \"Stop\"\n   180→\n   181→\n   182→def handle_pretool(data: dict) -> None:\n   183→    state = load_state()\n   184→    state[\"turn_count\"] = state.get(\"turn_count\", 0) + 1\n   185→\n   186→    tool_name = data.get(\"tool_name\", \"\")\n   187→    tool_input = data.get(\"tool_input\", {})\n   188→\n   189→    # Track file edits for loop detection\n   190→    if tool_name in (\"Edit\", \"Write\"):\n   191→        filepath = tool_input.get(\"file_path\", \"unknown\")\n   192→        state[\"file_edits\"][filepath] = state[\"file_edits\"].get(filepath, 0) + 1\n   193→\n   194→    # Track verification attempts\n   195→    if tool_name == \"Bash\":\n   196→        cmd = tool_input.get(\"command\", \"\")\n   197→        if is_verification_command(cmd):\n   198→            if not state.get(\"verification_attempted\"):\n   199→                # Reset stop counter when entering verification phase\n   200→                state[\"stop_block_count\"] = 0\n   201→            state[\"verification_attempted\"] = True\n   202→\n   203→    save_state(state)\n   204→    emit_allow(\"[tbench]\")\n   205→\n   206→\n   207→def handle_posttool(data: dict) -> None:\n   208→    state = load_state()\n   209→\n   210→    tool_name = data.get(\"tool_name\", \"\")\n   211→    tool_output = str(data.get(\"tool_output\", \"\"))\n   212→\n   213→    if tool_name == \"Bash\":\n   214→        # Detect errors\n   215→        has_error = any(err in tool_output for err in ERROR_PATTERNS)\n   216→\n   217→        if has_error:\n   218→            sig = tool_output[:200]\n   219→            if sig == state.get(\"last_error_sig\", \"\"):\n   220→                state[\"consecutive_failures\"] = state.get(\"consecutive_failures\", 0) + 1\n   221→            else:\n   222→                state[\"consecutive_failures\"] = 1\n   223→                state[\"last_error_sig\"] = sig\n   224→        else:\n   225→            state[\"consecutive_failures\"] = 0\n   226→\n   227→        # Detect verification success\n   228→        if state.get(\"verification_attempted\"):\n   229→            if any(kw in tool_output for kw in SUCCESS_PATTERNS):\n   230→                state[\"verification_passed\"] = True\n   231→\n   232→    save_state(state)\n   233→\n   234→    # Emit warnings for loops\n   235→    notifications = []\n   236→\n   237→    for filepath, count in state.get(\"file_edits\", {}).items():\n   238→        if count >= 4:\n   239→            notifications.append(\n   240→                f\"[tbench] WARNING: {filepath} edited {count} times. \"\n   241→                \"STOP editing this file. Re-read the requirements and try \"\n   242→                \"a fundamentally different approach.\"\n   243→            )\n   244→\n   245→    if state.get(\"consecutive_failures\", 0) >= 3:\n   246→        notifications.append(\n   247→            f\"[tbench] WARNING: {state['consecutive_failures']} consecutive failures \"\n   248→            \"with the same error. Your current approach is wrong. Switch strategy entirely.\"\n   249→        )\n   250→\n   251→    turn_count = state.get(\"turn_count\", 0)\n   252→    if turn_count >= 100 and not state.get(\"verification_attempted\"):\n   253→        notifications.append(\n   254→            f\"[tbench] CRITICAL: Turn {turn_count}/200 and you haven't run verification yet. \"\n   255→            \"STOP what you're doing. Run the tests NOW. Fix only what fails.\"\n   256→        )\n   257→    elif turn_count >= 40 and not state.get(\"verification_attempted\"):\n   258→        notifications.append(\n   259→            f\"[tbench] WARNING: Turn {turn_count}/200. Run verification NOW to check progress. \"\n   260→            \"Early feedback prevents wasted turns.\"\n   261→        )\n   262→    elif turn_count >= 150:\n   263→        notifications.append(\n   264→            f\"[tbench] WARNING: Turn {turn_count}/200. You are running low on turns. \"\n   265→            \"Wrap up — ensure tests pass and stop.\"\n   266→        )\n   267→    elif turn_count >= 120 and not state.get(\"verification_passed\"):\n   268→        notifications.append(\n   269→            f\"[tbench] WARNING: Turn {turn_count}/200. Tests not yet passing. \"\n   270→            \"Simplify your approach. A minimal passing solution beats a failed ambitious one.\"\n   271→        )\n   272→\n   273→    if notifications:\n   274→        emit_notification(\"PostToolUse\", \" | \".join(notifications))\n   275→    else:\n   276→        emit({})\n   277→\n   278→\n   279→def handle_stop(data: dict) -> None:\n   280→    state = load_state()\n   281→\n   282→    if not state.get(\"verification_attempted\", False):\n   283→        state[\"stop_block_count\"] = state.get(\"stop_block_count\", 0) + 1\n   284→        save_state(state)\n   285→\n   286→        # After 3 blocks, let it go (avoid infinite loop)\n   287→        if state[\"stop_block_count\"] >= 3:\n   288→            emit({})\n   289→            return\n   290→\n   291→        emit_notification(\n   292→            \"Stop\",\n   293→            \"[tbench] BLOCKED: You have NOT run the test suite. \"\n   294→            \"You MUST verify before stopping. Run: \"\n   295→            \"cd /app && bash tests/test.sh \"\n   296→            \"OR: cd /app && python3 -m pytest tests/ -v\"\n   297→        )\n   298→    elif not state.get(\"verification_passed\", False):\n   299→        state[\"stop_block_count\"] = state.get(\"stop_block_count\", 0) + 1\n   300→        save_state(state)\n   301→\n   302→        if state[\"stop_block_count\"] >= 2:\n   303→            emit({})\n   304→            return\n   305→\n   306→        emit_notification(\n   307→            \"Stop\",\n   308→            \"[tbench] Tests python3\n\"\"\"\nTerminal-Bench Claude Code Hooks (unified script).\n\nHandles all three hook events via stdin payload inspection:\n- PreToolUse: tracks state, always allows\n- PostToolUse: tracks errors, verification results, loop detection\n- Stop: blocks exit if verification hasn't been attempted\n\nProtocol:\n  Input: JSON via stdin\n  Output: JSON via stdout wrapped in {\"hookSpecificOutput\": {...}}\n\nState persisted in /tmp/.tbench_state.json across invocations.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport json\nimport sys\nimport time\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nSTATE_FILE = Path(\"/tmp/.tbench_state.json\")\n\n# Patterns that indicate the agent is running verification tests\nVERIFICATION_PATTERNS = [\n    \"tests/test.sh\",\n    \"tests/test_\",\n    \"pytest tests/\",\n    \"pytest -v\",\n    \"python -m pytest\",\n    \"python3 -m pytest\",\n    \"make test\",\n    \"cargo test\",\n    \"npm test\",\n    \"go test\",\n    \"bash test\",\n    \"./test.sh\",\n    \"ctest\",\n]\n\n# Patterns that indicate test success\nSUCCESS_PATTERNS = [\n    \"passed\",\n    \" ok\",\n    \"0 failed\",\n    \"tests passed\",\n    \"all tests\",\n    \"PASS\",\n    \"OK (\",\n    \"0 errors\",\n    \"Build succeeded\",\n    \"All checks passed\",\n]\n\n# Patterns that indicate errors in command output\nERROR_PATTERNS = [\n    \"error:\",\n    \"Error:\",\n    \"ERROR:\",\n    \"traceback\",\n    \"Traceback\",\n    \"FAILED\",\n    \"not found\",\n    \"No such file\",\n    \"permission denied\",\n    \"Permission denied\",\n    \"command not found\",\n    \"ModuleNotFoundError\",\n    \"ImportError\",\n    \"SyntaxError\",\n    \"NameError\",\n    \"TypeError\",\n    \"ValueError\",\n    \"FileNotFoundError\",\n    \"ConnectionRefusedError\",\n    \"OSError\",\n    \"RuntimeError\",\n    \"segmentation fault\",\n    \"Segmentation fault\",\n    \"core dumped\",\n    \"killed\",\n    \"Killed\",\n    \"Critical error:\",\n    \"AssertionError\",\n]\n\n# Common build artifacts that should be cleaned up\nBUILD_ARTIFACT_PATTERNS = [\n    \"a.out\",\n    \"*.o\",\n    \"*.obj\",\n    \"*.exe\",\n    \"*.dll\",\n    \"*.so\",\n    \"*.dylib\",\n    \"*.pyc\",\n    \"__pycache__\",\n    \".DS_Store\",\n    \"*.swp\",\n    \"*.swo\",\n    \"core\",\n    \"*.core\",\n]\n\n# Deliverable patterns - files that should NOT be cleaned\nDELIVERABLE_PATTERNS = [\n    \"main.py.c\",\n    \"main.c.py\",\n    \"move.txt\",\n    \"regex.txt\",\n    \"response.txt\",\n    \"solution.py\",\n    \"answer.txt\",\n    \"output.json\",\n    \"result.csv\",\n]\n\n\ndef load_state() -> dict:\n    if STATE_FILE.exists():\n        try:\n            return json.loads(STATE_FILE.read_text())\n        except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):\n            pass\n    return {\n        \"start_time\": time.time(),\n        \"turn_count\": 0,\n        \"file_edits\": {},\n        \"verification_attempted\": False,\n        \"verification_passed\": False,\n        \"consecutive_failures\": 0,\n        \"last_error_sig\": \"\",\n        \"stop_block_count\": 0,\n    }\n\n\ndef save_state(state: dict) -> None:\n    try:\n        STATE_FILE.write_text(json.dumps(state))\n    except OSError:\n        pass\n\n\ndef emit(payload: dict) -> None:\n    json.dump(payload, sys.stdout)\n\n\ndef emit_allow(reason: str = \"\") -> None:\n    emit({\n        \"hookSpecificOutput\": {\n            \"hookEventName\": \"PreToolUse\",\n            \"permissionDecision\": \"allow\",\n            \"permissionDecisionReason\": reason,\n        }\n    })\n\n\ndef emit_notification(event: str, message: str) -> None:\n    emit({\n        \"hookSpecificOutput\": {\n            \"hookEventName\": event,\n            \"notification\": message,\n        }\n    })\n\n\ndef is_verification_command(cmd: str) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Check if a command is running verification tests.\"\"\"\n    return any(pattern in cmd for pattern in VERIFICATION_PATTERNS)\n\n\ndef detect_hook_type(data: dict) -> str:\n    if \"tool_output\" in data:\n        return \"PostToolUse\"\n    if \"tool_name\" in data:\n        return \"PreToolUse\"\n    return \"Stop\"\n\n\ndef handle_pretool(data: dict) -> None:\n    state = load_state()\n    state[\"turn_count\"] = state.get(\"turn_count\", 0) + 1\n\n    tool_name = data.get(\"tool_name\", \"\")\n    tool_input = data.get(\"tool_input\", {})\n\n    # Track file edits for loop detection\n    if tool_name in (\"Edit\", \"Write\"):\n        filepath = tool_input.get(\"file_path\", \"unknown\")\n        state[\"file_edits\"][filepath] = state[\"file_edits\"].get(filepath, 0) + 1\n\n    # Track verification attempts\n    if tool_name == \"Bash\":\n        cmd = tool_input.get(\"command\", \"\")\n        if is_verification_command(cmd):\n            if not state.get(\"verification_attempted\"):\n                # Reset stop counter when entering verification phase\n                state[\"stop_block_count\"] = 0\n            state[\"verification_attempted\"] = True\n\n    save_state(state)\n    emit_allow(\"[tbench]\")\n\n\ndef handle_posttool(data: dict) -> None:\n    state = load_state()\n\n    tool_name = data.get(\"tool_name\", \"\")\n    tool_output = str(data.get(\"tool_output\", \"\"))\n\n    if tool_name == \"Bash\":\n        # Detect errors\n        has_error = any(err in tool_output for err in ERROR_PATTERNS)\n\n        if has_error:\n            sig = tool_output[:200]\n            if sig == state.get(\"last_error_sig\", \"\"):\n                state[\"consecutive_failures\"] = state.get(\"consecutive_failures\", 0) + 1\n            else:\n                state[\"consecutive_failures\"] = 1\n                state[\"last_error_sig\"] = sig\n        else:\n            state[\"consecutive_failures\"] = 0\n\n        # Detect verification success\n        if state.get(\"verification_attempted\"):\n            if any(kw in tool_output for kw in SUCCESS_PATTERNS):\n                state[\"verification_passed\"] = True\n\n    save_state(state)\n\n    # Emit warnings for loops\n    notifications = []\n\n    for filepath, count in state.get(\"file_edits\", {}).items():\n        if count >= 4:\n            notifications.append(\n                f\"[tbench] WARNING: {filepath} edited {count} times. \"\n                \"STOP editing this file. Re-read the requirements and try \"\n                \"a fundamentally different approach.\"\n            )\n\n    if state.get(\"consecutive_failures\", 0) >= 3:\n        notifications.append(\n            f\"[tbench] WARNING: {state['consecutive_failures']} consecutive failures \"\n            \"with the same error. Your current approach is wrong. Switch strategy entirely.\"\n        )\n\n    turn_count = state.get(\"turn_count\", 0)\n    if turn_count >= 100 and not state.get(\"verification_attempted\"):\n        notifications.append(\n            f\"[tbench] CRITICAL: Turn {turn_count}/200 and you haven't run verification yet. \"\n            \"STOP what you're doing. Run the tests NOW. Fix only what fails.\"\n        )\n    elif turn_count >= 40 and not state.get(\"verification_attempted\"):\n        notifications.append(\n            f\"[tbench] WARNING: Turn {turn_count}/200. Run verification NOW to check progress. \"\n            \"Early feedback prevents wasted turns.\"\n        )\n    elif turn_count >= 150:\n        notifications.append(\n            f\"[tbench] WARNING: Turn {turn_count}/200. You are running low on turns. \"\n            \"Wrap up — ensure tests pass and stop.\"\n        )\n    elif turn_count >= 120 and not state.get(\"verification_passed\"):\n        notifications.append(\n            f\"[tbench] WARNING: Turn {turn_count}/200. Tests not yet passing. \"\n            \"Simplify your approach. A minimal passing solution beats a failed ambitious one.\"\n        )\n\n    if notifications:\n        emit_notification(\"PostToolUse\", \" | \".join(notifications))\n    else:\n        emit({})\n\n\ndef handle_stop(data: dict) -> None:\n    state = load_state()\n\n    if not state.get(\"verification_attempted\", False):\n        state[\"stop_block_count\"] = state.get(\"stop_block_count\", 0) + 1\n        save_state(state)\n\n        # After 3 blocks, let it go (avoid infinite loop)\n        if state[\"stop_block_count\"] >= 3:\n            emit({})\n            return\n\n        emit_notification(\n            \"Stop\",\n            \"[tbench] BLOCKED: You have NOT run the test suite. \"\n            \"You MUST verify before stopping. Run: \"\n            \"cd /app && bash tests/test.sh \"\n            \"OR: cd /app && python3 -m pytest tests/ -v\"\n        )\n    elif not state.get(\"verification_passed\", False):\n        state[\"stop_block_count\"] = state.get(\"stop_block_count\", 0) + 1\n        save_state(state)\n\n        if state[\"stop_block_count\"] >= 2:\n            emit({})\n            return\n\n        emit_notification(\n            \"Stop\",\n            \"[tbench] Tests were run but appear to have failed. \"\n            \"Review the test output, fix issues, and re-run verification. \"\n            \"If you believe tests actually passed, you may stop.\"\n        )\n    else:\n        emit({})\n\n\ndef main() -> None:\n    try:\n        data = json.load(sys.stdin)\n    except (json.JSONDecodeError, EOFError):\n        data = {}\n\n    hook_type = detect_hook_type(data)\n\n    if hook_type == \"PreToolUse\":\n        handle_pretool(data)\n    elif hook_type == \"PostToolUse\":\n        handle_posttool(data)\n    elif hook_type == \"Stop\":\n        handle_stop(data)\n    else:\n        emit({})\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    main()\n","numLines":336,"startLine":1,"totalLines":336}}}

The tests will likely be created by the harness during verification. Based on the CLAUDE.md instructions (rule #32 about XSS filtering), I know the key constraints:\n\n1. XSS vectors must not trigger `alert()` in a headless browser\n2. Clean HTML must pass through **byte-identical** (no whitespace changes, no attribute reordering, no entity conversion)\n3. Do NOT use bleach, BeautifulSoup, or lxml — they normalize HTML\n4. Use a TWO-PASS regex/string approach that is SUBTRACTIVE — only removes dangerous content\n\nLet me create the filter.py script following the guidelines from the CLAUDE.md.\n\n\nI'll write a script that reads an HTML file, strips out script tags and event handlers using regex patterns, removes dangerous elements like iframes and embeds, and eliminates protocol-based attacks like javascript: and data: URLs, all while preserving the exact formatting of the remaining content. Writing the sanitization filter... I'm settling on a two-pass approach: first strip null bytes, then remove script tags entirely, strip all event handler attributes with a regex that handles various quote styles, delete dangerous tags like iframe and object, and finally sanitize href/src/action attributes by replacing javascript: and data: protocols with safe anchors.\n\nNow I need to handle the edge cases carefully—script tags can appear with or without attributes, self-closing variants, missing closing tags, and various case combinations. Same goes for other dangerous tags like iframe, which might be self-closing or left unclosed. I'll write a filter that accounts for all these variations.\n\nFor nested obfuscation attempts like `<scr<script>ipt>`, I'll need to loop the removal process until no more matches appear. I'm also considering other XSS vectors like event handlers in attributes, javascript: URLs, and CSS-based attacks, though most modern browsers have mitigated the CSS ones. The event handler and URL sanitization should catch those cases.\n\nStill writing the filter... Writing the XSS filter... Continuing with the sanitization logic...\n\nI'm extracting the attribute name, quotes, and URL value from the match, then checking if the URL contains dangerous protocols like javascript, vbscript, or data by stripping HTML entities and whitespace. If it's malicious, I replace it with a safe placeholder, otherwise I keep the original attribute intact.\n\nNow I'm reconsidering the regex pattern itself—it needs to properly match href, src, action, and formaction attributes whether they're quoted with double quotes, single quotes, or unquoted, and I'm working through the edge cases to make sure the backreference correctly matches opening and closing quotes.\n\nLet me refactor this with a cleaner approach that replaces dangerous URLs like javascript: and data: with a safe # anchor instead, and I'm building a function that strips whitespace and decodes HTML entities to check if the URL matches a dangerous protocol pattern.\n\nStill writing sanitization logic... I'm realizing the regex approach is getting unwieldy with all these patterns. Let me reconsider the `data:` protocol—it's legitimately used for inline images, but `data:text/html` can enable XSS attacks. Since I'm already stripping iframes, I could block just the HTML variant while preserving image data URLs, though the safest approach might be to block all data URLs entirely to keep things simple.\n\nLooking back at the guidance, I should follow the recommendation to replace `javascript:`, `data:`, and `vbscript:` URLs with `#`. For the event handler regex, I need to account for various quote styles and unquoted attribute values to catch onclick, onload, and similar handlers.\n\nNow I'm realizing the `on\\w+` pattern might be too broad—it could match legitimate attributes like `onion` that just happen to start with \"on\". I should probably be more specific and only target known event handler names, or accept that this is an acceptable trade-off for catching XSS vectors.\n\nI should also handle `<meta>` refresh tags that use `javascript:` URLs, and I'm confident the other common XSS patterns like img onerror handlers and details ontoggle are already covered by removing event attributes. I need to handle HTML entity encoding in URLs too—things like `&#106;avascript:alert(1)` where entities encode parts of the protocol. Rather than decoding entities in the output (which would change the HTML), I'll decode them only for validation checks to catch these obfuscated attempts.\n\nI'm using Python's `html.unescape()` to decode the URL for checking, then stripping whitespace and converting to lowercase before testing if it starts with dangerous protocols like `javascript:`, `vbscript:`, or `data:`. This way I catch obfuscated variants without modifying the actual output.\n\nI also need to account for tricks like embedded tabs, newlines, and null bytes in the protocol name—the whitespace removal handles those. URL encoding like `%6A` for 'j' isn't automatically decoded by the browser's HTML parser in href attributes, so that's less of a concern, but I should verify where the actual vulnerability lies.\n\nHTML entities in URLs are decoded before execution though, so my `html.unescape` approach should catch those cases. I'm also considering edge cases like `<noscript>` tags, which should stay since they're legitimate, and `<template>` tags that contain inactive scripts—those might need special handling since they're not executed until JavaScript activates them.\n\nNow I'm ready to implement the sanitizer. {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_015JVacdGvPDwmVUmGeTGvJX","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_015do7AA5cAHkeKGErxPTgFR","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/app/filter.py","content":"#!/usr/bin/env python3\n\"\"\"XSS filter - removes JavaScript from HTML while preserving formatting.\"\"\"\n\nimport html as html_module\nimport re\nimport sys\n\n\ndef filter_html(content):\n    # Strip NULL bytes\n    content = content.replace('\\x00', '')\n\n    # Remove <script>...</script> tags (loop for nested/obfuscated attempts)\n    prev = None\n    while prev != content:\n        prev = content\n        content = re.sub(\n            r'<script\\b[^>]*>.*?</script\\s*>',\n            '', content, flags=re.DOTALL | re.IGNORECASE\n        )\n\n    # Remove remaining unclosed/self-closing <script> tags and stray </script>\n    content = re.sub(r'<script\\b[^>]*/?\\s*>', '', content, flags=re.IGNORECASE)\n    content = re.sub(r'</script\\s*>', '', content, flags=re.IGNORECASE)\n\n    # Remove <style>...</style> tags (CSS can contain expression()/javascript: XSS)\n    prev = None\n    while prev != content:\n        prev = content\n        content = re.sub(\n            r'<style\\b[^>]*>.*?</style\\s*>',\n            '', content, flags=re.DOTALL | re.IGNORECASE\n        )\n    content = re.sub(r'<style\\b[^>]*/?\\s*>', '', content, flags=re.IGNORECASE)\n    content = re.sub(r'</style\\s*>', '', content, flags=re.IGNORECASE)\n\n    # Remove dangerous container tags with their content\n    for tag in ['iframe', 'object', 'embed', 'applet', 'svg', 'math']:\n        prev = None\n        while prev != content:\n            prev = content\n            content = re.sub(\n                rf'<{tag}\\b[^>]*>.*?</{tag}\\s*>',\n                '', content, flags=re.DOTALL | re.IGNORECASE\n            )\n        content = re.sub(rf'<{tag}\\b[^>]*/?\\s*>', '', content, flags=re.IGNORECASE)\n        content = re.sub(rf'</{tag}\\s*>', '', content, flags=re.IGNORECASE)\n\n    # Remove <base> tags (can redirect all relative URLs)\n    content = re.sub(r'<base\\b[^>]*/?\\s*>', '', content, flags=re.IGNORECASE)\n\n    # Remove ALL event handler attributes (on*)\n    content = re.sub(\n        r'\\s+on\\w+\\s*=\\s*(?:\"[^\"]*\"|\\'[^\\']*\\'|[^\\s>]*)',\n        '', content, flags=re.IGNORECASE\n    )\n\n    # Sanitize dangerous URLs in href/src/action/formaction attributes\n    def sanitize_url(match):\n        prefix = match.group(1)\n        quote_char = match.group(2)\n        url = match.group(3)\n\n        # Decode HTML entities and strip control chars for protocol check\n        url_check = html_module.unescape(url)\n        url_check = re.sub(r'[\\s\\x00-\\x1f]+', '', url_check).lower()\n\n        if re.match(r'(javascript|vbscript|data)\\s*:', url_check):\n            return f'{prefix}{quote_char}#{quote_char}'\n        return match.group(0)\n\n    # Double-quoted attribute values\n    content = re.sub(\n        r'((?:href|src|action|formaction)\\s*=\\s*)(\")([^\"]*)\"',\n        sanitize_url, content, flags=re.IGNORECASE\n    )\n    # Single-quoted attribute values\n    content = re.sub(\n        r\"((?:href|src|action|formaction)\\s*=\\s*)(')([^']*)'\",\n        sanitize_url, content, flags=re.IGNORECASE\n    )\n    # Unquoted attribute values\n    content = re.sub(\n        r'((?:href|src|action|formaction)\\s*=\\s*)()([^\\s>]+)',\n        sanitize_url, content, flags=re.IGNORECASE\n    )\n\n    return content\n\n\ndef main():\n    if len(sys.argv) < 2:\n        print(\"Usage: filter.py <html_file>\", file=sys.stderr)\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    filepath = sys.argv[1]\n\n    with open(filepath, 'r', encoding='utf-8', errors='replace') as f:\n        content = f.read()\n\n    content = filter_html(content)\n\n    with open(filepath, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:\n        f.write(content)\n\n\nif __name__ == '__main__':\n    main()\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":215,"cache_read_input_tokens":28198,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":215,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":8,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"babed27f-3f4e-4d62-92c2-9860a848a143","uuid":"bbe8cdf0-de4c-429c-a9f7-ac3f0a485709"}

Sanitize dangerous URLs in href/src/action/formaction attributes\n    def sanitize_url(match):\n        prefix = match.group(1)\n        quote_char = match.group(2)\n        url = match.group(3)\n\n        # Decode HTML entities and strip control chars for protocol check\n        url_check = html_module.unescape(url)\n        url_check = re.sub(r'[\\s\\x00-\\x1f]+', '', url_check).lower()\n\n        if re.match(r'(javascript|vbscript|data)\\s*:', url_check):\n            return f'{prefix}{quote_char}#{quote_char}'\n        return match.group(0)\n\n    # Double-quoted attribute values\n    content = re.sub(\n        r'((?:href|src|action|formaction)\\s*=\\s*)(\")([^\"]*)\"',\n        sanitize_url, content, flags=re.IGNORECASE\n    )\n    # Single-quoted attribute values\n    content = re.sub(\n        r\"((?:href|src|action|formaction)\\s*=\\s*)(')([^']*)'\",\n        sanitize_url, content, flags=re.IGNORECASE\n    )\n    # Unquoted attribute values\n    content = re.sub(\n        r'((?:href|src|action|formaction)\\s*=\\s*)()([^\\s>]+)',\n        sanitize_url, content, flags=re.IGNORECASE\n    )\n\n    return content\n\n\ndef main():\n    if len(sys.argv) < 2:\n        print(\"Usage: filter.py <html_file>\", file=sys.stderr)\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    filepath = sys.argv[1]\n\n    with open(filepath, 'r', encoding='utf-8', errors='replace') as f:\n        content = f.read()\n\n    content = filter_html(content)\n\n    with open(filepath, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:\n        f.write(content)\n\n\nif __name__ == '__main__':\n    main()\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null}}

'<script' not in result.lower(), f'Script not removed: {result}'\nassert '<p>Hello</p>' in result and '<p>World</p>' in result\nprint('PASS: Script tags removed')\n\n# Test 3: Event handlers removed\nhtml = '<img src=\\\"photo.jpg\\\" onerror=\\\"alert(1)\\\">'\nresult = filter_html(html)\nassert 'onerror' not in result.lower(), f'Event handler not removed: {result}'\nassert 'src=\\\"photo.jpg\\\"' in result\nprint('PASS: Event handlers removed')\n\n# Test 4: javascript: URLs sanitized\nhtml = '<a href=\\\"javascript:alert(1)\\\">Link</a>'\nresult = filter_html(html)\nassert 'javascript' not in result.lower(), f'javascript: URL not sanitized: {result}'\nassert '<a href=\\\"#\\\">Link</a>' == result\nprint('PASS: javascript: URLs sanitized')\n\n# Test 5: Complex clean HTML preserved\nclean = '''<table border=\\\"1\\\">\n  <tr><th>Name</th><th>Age</th></tr>\n  <tr><td>Alice</td><td>30</td></tr>\n</table>'''\nresult = filter_html(clean)\nassert src=\\\"evil.html\\\"></iframe><div>After</div>'\nresult = filter_html(html)\nassert '<iframe' not in result.lower()\nassert '<div>Before</div>' in result and '<div>After</div>' in result\nprint('PASS: iframe removed')\n\n# Test 7: SVG with script removed\nhtml = '<p>Hi</p><svg onload=\\\"alert(1)\\\"><script>alert(2)</script></svg><p>Bye</p>'\nresult = filter_html(html)\nassert '<svg' not in result.lower() and '<script' not in result.lower()\nprint('PASS: SVG removed')\n\n# Test 8: Entity-encoded javascript: URL\nhtml = '<a href=\\\"&#106;avascript:alert(1)\\\">Link</a>'\nresult = filter_html(html)\nassert 'alert' not in result, f'Entity-encoded JS not caught: {result}'\nprint('PASS: Entity-encoded javascript: URL sanitized')\n\n# Test 9: Inline event handler with single quotes\nhtml = \\\"<div onclick='alert(1)'>Click</div>\\\"\nresult = filter_html(html)\nassert 'onclick' not in result\nprint('PASS: {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01ME9u3aUmMdiPGt8MtiQXpp","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_0161oKyRpnsTrjjJut1S8k8m","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"# Test command-line interface\ncat > /tmp/test_xss.html << 'EOF'\n<!DOCTYPE html>\n<html>\n<head><title>Test Page</title></head>\n<body>\n<h1>Welcome</h1>\n<p class=\"intro\">This is a <strong>test</strong> page.</p>\n<table border=\"1\">\n  <tr><th>Item</th><th>Price</th></tr>\n  <tr><td>Apple</td><td>$1.00</td></tr>\n</table>\n<script>alert('XSS')</script>\n<img src=\"photo.jpg\" onerror=\"alert('xss')\">\n<a href=\"javascript:void(0)\">Bad Link</a>\n<a href=\"https://example.com\">Good Link</a>\n<div onclick=\"doEvil()\">Click me</div>\n<iframe src=\"evil.html\"></iframe>\n<p>End of page.</p>\n</body>\n</html>\nEOF\n\ncp /tmp/test_xss.html /tmp/test_xss_backup.html\npython3 /app/filter.py /tmp/test_xss.html\necho \"=== FILTERED OUTPUT ===\"\ncat /tmp/test_xss.html\necho \"\"\necho \"=== VERIFY NO JS ===\"\ngrep -i 'script\\|alert\\|onclick\\|onerror\\|javascript\\|iframe' /tmp/test_xss.html || echo \"No JS found - PASS\"\n","description":"Test CLI interface and verify output"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1039,"cache_read_input_tokens":41398,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":1039,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"babed27f-3f4e-4d62-92c2-9860a848a143","uuid":"097b13aa-6f9b-4e1e-b59f-f06a057ac794"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01Dr7sLnSNi2RRfFD6pChUGF","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01S5rE6DYupxrNtUHTECbMKx","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"python3 -c \"\nfrom filter import filter_html\n\n# Edge cases\ntests = [\n    # Nested script trick\n    ('<scr<script>ipt>alert(1)</scr</script>ipt>', 'nested script'),\n    # Case variations\n    ('<SCRIPT>alert(1)</SCRIPT>', 'uppercase script'),\n    ('<ScRiPt>alert(1)</sCrIpT>', 'mixed case script'),\n    # Multiline script\n    ('<script>\\nalert(1)\\n</script>', 'multiline script'),\n    # Script with attributes\n    ('<script type=\\\"text/javascript\\\" src=\\\"evil.js\\\"></script>', 'script with attrs'),\n    # Null bytes\n    ('<scr\\x00ipt>alert(1)</script>', 'null byte in script'),\n    # vbscript URL\n    ('<a href=\\\"vbscript:MsgBox(1)\\\">x</a>', 'vbscript URL'),\n    # data: URL\n    ('<a href=\\\"data:text/html,<script>alert(1)</script>\\\">x</a>', 'data URL'),\n    # Whitespace in javascript: URL\n    ('<a href=\\\"  javascript:alert(1)\\\">x</a>', 'whitespace before javascript:'),\n    # Unquoted event handler\n    ('<img src=x onerror=alert(1)>', 'unquoted event handler'),\n    # Object tag\n    ('<object data=\\\"evil.swf\\\"></object>', 'object tag'),\n    # Embed tag\n    ('<embed src=\\\"evil.swf\\\">', 'embed tag'),\n    # Base tag\n    ('<base href=\\\"http://evil.com/\\\">', 'base tag'),\n    # SVG with event\n    ('<svg><rect onmouseover=\\\"alert(1)\\\"/></svg>', 'svg with event'),\n    # Math tag\n    ('<math><maction actiontype=\\\"statusline\\\">x</maction></math>', 'math tag'),\n]\n\nfor html, name in tests:\n    result = filter_html(html)\n    has_danger = any(x in result.lower() for x in ['<script', 'alert', 'onerror', 'onclick', 'onmouseover', 'javascript:', 'vbscript:', '<iframe', '<object', '<embed', '<svg', '<math', '<base'])\n    status = 'PASS' if not has_danger else 'FAIL'\n    print(f'{status}: {name} => {result!r}')\n\"\n","description":"Test edge cases for XSS filter"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":659,"cache_read_input_tokens":42437,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":659,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"babed27f-3f4e-4d62-92c2-9860a848a143","uuid":"7a1abac6-b98b-4a35-a5ee-6cff3593fcad"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_016FMaeyWPbbfC6RTx4GADCr","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01J1Hkk9wL3teBoq36vXtgpp","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"python3 -c \"\nfrom filter import filter_html\n\n# Verify byte-for-byte preservation of clean HTML\ntests = [\n    '<p class=\\\"x\\\">Hello &amp; welcome</p>',\n    '<img src=\\\"photo.jpg\\\" alt=\\\"A photo\\\">',\n    '<a href=\\\"https://example.com?a=1&b=2\\\">Link</a>',\n    '<input type=\\\"text\\\" value=\\\"Enter name\\\" disabled>',\n    '<div id=\\\"main\\\" class=\\\"container\\\" data-value=\\\"42\\\">Content</div>',\n    '<br>',\n    '<hr/>',\n    '<!-- This is a comment -->',\n    '<pre>  spaces   preserved  </pre>',\n    '<h1>Title</h1>\\n<h2>Subtitle</h2>',\n    '<p>&copy; 2024 Company</p>',\n    '<td colspan=\\\"2\\\">Spanning cell</td>',\n    '<form action=\\\"/submit\\\" method=\\\"post\\\"><input type=\\\"submit\\\"></form>',\n    '<link rel=\\\"stylesheet\\\" href=\\\"style.css\\\">',\n]\n\nall_pass = True\nfor html in tests:\n    result = filter_html(html)\n    if result != html:\n        print(f'FAIL: {html!r} => {result!r}')\n        all_pass = False\n    else:\n        print(f'PASS: preserved {html[:50]}...' if len(html) > 50 else f'PASS: preserved {html}')\n\nif all_pass:\n    print('\\nAll preservation tests passed!')\n\"\n","description":"Verify clean HTML is preserved byte-for-byte"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":881,"cache_read_input_tokens":43096,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":881,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"babed27f-3f4e-4d62-92c2-9860a848a143","uuid":"679ebdea-5117-4e2b-aa3c-3267fcfd5623"}

The `/app/filter.py` script:\n\n- **Takes an HTML file as `argv[1]`** and modifies it in-place\n- **Removes JavaScript** by stripping:\n  - `<script>` tags and their content (handles nested, multiline, case variations)\n  - Event handler attributes (`onclick`, `onerror`, `onload`, etc.)\n  - `javascript:`, `vbscript:`, and `data:` URLs in `href`/`src`/`action`/`formaction` (including HTML entity-encoded variants)\n  - `<style>` tags (CSS `expression()` XSS vector)\n  - Dangerous embedding tags: `<iframe>`, `<object>`, `<embed>`, `<applet>`, `<svg>`, `<math>`, `<base>`\n  - NULL bytes\n- **Preserves clean HTML byte-for-byte** — uses a subtractive regex approach that only removes dangerous content without reconstructing/normalizing the HTML"}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":846,"cache_read_input_tokens":43977,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":846,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"babed27f-3f4e-4d62-92c2-9860a848a143","uuid":"eeafe1d6-664f-4127-9137-050539975a96"}
